title: Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia in the Neonate
authors: Schmidt AF, Bancalari EH / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia?
BPD คือ โรคปอดเรื้อรังที่พบบ่อยที่สุดในทารกคลอดก่อนกำหนด — ยังคงเป็น most important respiratory complication ของ preterm birth แม้ผ่านมากกว่า 50 ปี นับตั้งแต่ Northway บรรยายครั้งแรกใน 1967. (1) ทารก <28 wk มีอุบัติการณ์ BPD ใกล้ 40% (NICHD NRN 2013–2018) — ทารกที่เราดูแลในหอผู้ป่วย NICU เกือบครึ่งหนึ่งจะออกจาก NICU ด้วย BPD; (2) BPD ไม่ใช่แค่ปัญหาระยะสั้น — lung function trajectory ถูก set ตั้งแต่ early life, และ preterm BPD survivors มี impaired lung function ไปจนถึงวัยรุ่น-ผู้ใหญ่ (Northway cohort NEJM 1990, EPICure 2010); (3) BPD complicates everything ใน NICU: feeding, growth, PH, neuro outcome, discharge planning; (4) 54% ของ severe BPD survivors ต้องการ special education ที่อายุ 8 ปี (Short Pediatrics 2003); (5) มีช่องว่าง center variation กว้าง — incidence ต่างกันหลายเท่า (Lapcharoensap JAMA Pediatr 2015) — ทักษะ neonatologist เปลี่ยน outcome ได้จริง. เราต้องรู้ pathogenesis, การให้ definition ที่ถูกต้อง, prevention ที่มีหลักฐาน, และ management ที่ครบ.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
บรรยาย epidemiology ของ BPD: อุบัติการณ์ตาม GA, แนวโน้ม 1993–2018, center variation, และเหตุที่ incidence ไม่ลดลง
จำแนก BPD ตาม NICHD Workshop 2018 definition: mild/moderate/severe ตาม mode of support + FiO2 ที่ 36 wk PMA
อธิบาย pathogenesis: Old BPD vs New BPD — antenatal + postnatal risk factors, inflammation as final common pathway
อธิบาย pathology: characteristic morphologic changes — ↓ alveoli + ↓ capillaries = alveolar simplification (new BPD); emphysema + fibrosis (old BPD)
อธิบาย pulmonary function abnormalities ใน BPD: compliance, resistance, FRC, dead space, V/Q mismatch, CO2 retention
ระบุ prevention strategies ที่มี RCT/meta-analysis evidence พร้อม RR/OR และ strategies ที่ยัง inconclusive
วางแผน respiratory support ใน established BPD: target SpO2, Vt, PEEP, weaning strategy, bronchodilators, corticosteroids
อธิบาย pulmonary hypertension ใน BPD: pathophysiology, monitoring, treatment (iNO, sildenafil, nifedipine, pulmonary vein stenosis)
อธิบาย fluid/nutrition/infection management ใน BPD: diuretic complications, high-calorie nutrition, rickets prevention
วางแผน follow-up outcome: lung function trajectory ถึงวัยรุ่น, neuro sequelae, RSV prophylaxis (palivizumab), special education needs
วิเคราะห์ steroid dilemma: early dexamethasone vs hydrocortisone vs inhaled budesonide vs intratracheal budesonide/surfactant — evidence + risk threshold
รู้จัก emerging strategies: mesenchymal stem cells, LISA surfactant, lung MRI, microbiome — current evidence stage
1. ประวัติและ Epidemiology
1.1 Northway 1967 — BPD ครั้งแรก
Northway WH NEJM 1967;276:357: บรรยาย BPD ครั้งแรกใน infants ที่มี severe RDS → ต้องใช้ mechanical ventilation ที่ high airway pressure + high FiO2 เป็นเวลานาน → severe chronic lung damage →
ทารกกลุ่มนี้ล้วนมี severe respiratory failure; clinical + radiographic course จาก initial severe RDS → final chronic lung disease with cor pulmonale + mortality
ลักษณะ radiographic ของ Old BPD: hyperinflation + emphysema สลับกับ dense areas of atelectasis + fibrosis
1.2 Epidemiology ปัจจุบัน
NICHD NRN 2013–2018 (Bell EF JAMA 2022): incidence BPD ใน infants <28 wk ~40%; stable or slightly increasing trend เมื่อเทียบกับ 2008
เหตุที่ incidence ไม่ลด: ↑ survival ของ extremely preterm infants → มี infants ที่ vulnerable มากขึ้นรอดชีวิต; ทั้งนี้ส่วนใหญ่เป็น milder forms แทนที่จะเป็น severe BPD
Center variation: BPD incidence แตกต่างกันมากระหว่าง centers (Lapcharoensap JAMA Pediatr 2015;169:e143676) — เกิดจาก clinical practices + population + definition ที่ใช้
Risk factors สำคัญ: ↓ GA (most important), ↓ BW, male sex, White race, maternal smoking, maternal hypertension, IUGR, chorioamnionitis (ผล inconsistent), sepsis, severe RDS, PDA
2. Definition ของ BPD
2.1 Evolution ของ Definition
คำจำกัดความดั้งเดิม (Bancalari 1979): oxygen use + radiograph consistent with BPD ที่ 28 days

Jobe & Bancalari (2001 NIH Workshop): oxygen requirement ที่ 36 wk PMA = better predictor ของ long-term pulmonary outcomes กว่า 28 days; severity-based (mild/moderate/severe) ตาม duration + level ของ oxygen therapy
NICHD Workshop 2018 (Higgins RD J Pediatr 2018;197:300): revised definition — incorporate newer respiratory support modalities; severity based on
2.2 NICHD 2018 Definition (Table 70.1)

rows: Noninvasive PPV or NC ≥3 L/min @ 21%  OR  NC 1–3 L/min @ 22–29%  OR  NC <1 L/min @ 22–70%
Invasive PPV @ 21%  OR  Noninvasive PPV/NC ≥3 L/min @ 22–29%  OR  NC 1–3 L/min / hood @ ≥30%  OR  NC 1 L/min @ >70%
Invasive PPV @ >21%  OR  Noninvasive PPV/NC ≥3 L/min @ ≥30%

blocks.text: Assessment at 36 wk PMA (or discharge if <36 wk)

Limitations: (1) non-pulmonary causes of respiratory failure (central apnea, airway complications) อาจถูก classify เป็น BPD; (2) infants ที่ตายก่อน 36 wk ด้วย severe respiratory failure ถูก exclude; (3) ไม่ account for preterm infants ที่ will have abnormal lung function โดยไม่มี BPD diagnosis

New BPD vs Old BPD — Key Differences
Old BPD (1967 era): more mature infants (>28 wk); severe RDS → high pressure/high O2 ventilation; pathology: emphysema, fibrosis, alternating atelectasis; radiograph: hyperinflation + dense opacities; high mortality, cor pulmonale. New BPD (current): extremely preterm (<28 wk); late canalicular/early saccular stage at birth; milder initial respiratory failure; pathology: ↓ alveoli + ↓ capillaries = alveolar simplification (arrest of lung development); radiograph: diffuse haziness; lower mortality but significant long-term morbidity. Jobe AJ Pediatr Res 1999: coined 'an arrest of lung development' for new BPD.
3. Pathogenesis
3.1 Prenatal Risk Factors
Prematurity — most important: prevalence of BPD inversely related to GA + BW → abnormal lung development following preterm birth plays critical role
IUGR: impaired alveolar and vascular growth → ↑ BPD risk (Sehgal J Physiol 2019)
Chorioamnionitis: effect
หลาย studies: ↑ BPD risk; แต่บางส่วน no association
Effect ขึ้นกับ fetal inflammatory response, causative organism, severity, duration
Chorioamnionitis → spectrum: inflammation + lung injury → OR → enhanced lung maturation
Genetic predisposition: twin studies → genetic factors contribute to variance in BPD risk; GWAS ยังไม่ identify specific candidate genes
Maternal smoking, hypertension, maternal diabetes: ↑ BPD risk
3.2 Postnatal Risk Factors
Mechanical Trauma (Volutrauma)
High peak inspiratory pressure → lung damage; difficult to separate pressure effect จาก underlying lung disease

Key mechanism: excessive tidal volume (Hillman NH Am J Resp Crit Care Med 2007;176:575): large Vt ก่อน surfactant replacement → ↑ inflammatory cascade + interfere normal lung development in preterm lambs; companion study (Hillman Respir Res 2010) แสดง ↓ lung compliance ด้วย
Chronic noninjurious MV 6 days in preterm lambs → persistent alveolar simplification at 5 months (Dahl Am J Physiol 2018)
Gentler ventilation → less inflammation but still contributes to developmental alteration → milder BPD
Oxygen Toxicity
Lung exposed directly to highest PaO2 — toxic level depends on GA, nutritional/endocrine status, duration
Pathologic changes: atelectasis, edema, alveolar hemorrhage, inflammation, fibrin deposition, ↑ alveolar membrane thickness
Early capillary endothelium damage → plasma leaks into interstitial + alveolar spaces; surfactant inactivation by protein
Role diminished by: antenatal corticosteroids, surfactant therapy, careful oxygen titration
Postnatal Infection
Nosocomial infections: associated with marked ↑ risk of BPD, especially in very small infants (Kim PLoS One 2020)
Ureaplasma urealyticum: tracheal colonization at birth → ↑ BPD risk in VLBW; azithromycin eradicates ureaplasma but BPD outcome inconsistent (Viscardi Arch Dis Child 2020)
Airway microbiome dysbiosis: ↓ Lactobacilli + ↑ Proteobacteria/Firmicutes → progression of BPD (Pammi J Pediatr 2019); causal relationship not proven
PDA and Pulmonary Edema
Moderate-to-large PDA ≥7 days → OR for BPD (Clyman Am J Perinatol 2020;37:216): ↑ pulmonary blood flow → ↑ interstitial fluid → ↓ compliance + ↑ airway resistance → prolong MV → ↑ BPD risk
Mechanism of fluid accumulation in BPD: ↑ PVR, ↓ oncotic pressure, ↑ capillary permeability, LV dysfunction, ↑ O2, trauma, PDA, inflammation
Prospective clinical trials ของ early PDA closure: ไม่ show ↓ BPD incidence (Ohlsson Cochrane 2018)
Other Factors
Vitamin A deficiency: lower levels in BPD infants; vitamin A supplementation → small reduction in BPD (Darlow Cochrane 2016; RR 0.87 [0.77–0.99])

Early adrenal insufficiency: ↓ cortisol first week → ↑ PDA + ↑ lung inflammation + ↑ BPD (Watterberg Pediatrics 2000); early hydrocortisone → ↑ survival without BPD but ↑ sepsis in smallest (Baud Lancet 2016)
3.3 Final Common Pathway — Inflammation

Inflammation เป็น Final Common Pathway ของ BPD
Triggered by: O2, mechanical ventilation, PDA, infection. Evidence: ↑ inflammatory cells + proinflammatory cytokines (chemokines, IL, leukotrienes, PAF) in BPD infants; ↑ desmosine excretion (elastin degradation) ใน first week → predict BPD. Consequences: bronchoconstriction, vasoconstriction, ↑ vascular permeability, ↓ alveolar + capillary formation. Key morphologic result: ↓ alveolar number + gas exchange surface area + ↓ lung capillaries = hallmark of new BPD.
4. Pathology และ Clinical Presentation
4.1 Pathology

New BPD (mild, most common now): diffuse injury; little emphysema or fibrosis; reduced + simplified alveoli, decreased + dysmorphic capillaries, ↓ gas exchange surface area (Coalson J Semin Neonatol 2003; Fig 70.6)

Severe/Old-type BPD (minority, still present): areas of emphysema (coalescing → cystic), surrounded by atelectasis, interstitial edema, ↑ fibrous tissue, bronchial/bronchiolar mucosal hyperplasia + metaplasia; vascular changes of PH (medial muscle hypertrophy, elastic degeneration); RV hypertrophy ± LV hypertrophy
4.2 Pulmonary Function
Tracheobronchomalacia: develops in some → marked airway obstruction especially with agitation/↑ intrathoracic pressure
Chronic hypercapnia → ↑ serum HCO3 to compensate respiratory acidosis; exaggerated by loop diuretics
Airway hyperreactivity: smooth muscle hypertrophy → bronchoconstriction; hypoxia worsens airway resistance
4.3 Clinical Presentation
Most current infants: mild respiratory failure requiring CPAP or low-pressure/O2 ventilation → slow steady improvement → gradual weaning before discharge
Small group: atypical course → inadequate surfactant response → severe RDS → air leaks + secondary infections + recurrent pulmonary edema → severe BPD + PH
Feeding difficulty: chronic hypoxia + ↑ work of breathing → ↑ energy expenditure → weight gain < expected despite adequate calories
Differential diagnosis before concluding BPD: congenital heart disease, congenital pulmonary anomalies, chemical pneumonitis (recurrent aspiration), cystic fibrosis, surfactant homeostasis disorders
4.4 Imaging
Chest X-ray: most accessible; new BPD → diffuse haziness (interstitial pattern); old BPD → hyperinflation + emphysema + dense atelectasis

CT chest: better parenchymal resolution; prospective studies → CT findings correlate with duration O2 therapy + ventilatory impairment (van Mastrigt Pediatr Pulmonol 2017); radiation → reserved for severe BPD with differential
Lung ultrasound: bedside point-of-care; predictive of BPD (Mohamed J Pediatr 2021); limitations: operator-dependent, no standardized protocol, limited long-term outcome data

Lung MRI: ultra-short echo MRI → better parenchymal assessment + dynamic airway evaluation (tracheobronchomalacia); correlates with disease severity + predicts respiratory support at discharge (Higano J Perinatol 2021); hyperpolarized gas MRI: functional + microstructural assessment — research stage
5. Prevention of BPD
5.1 Strategies with RCT/Meta-Analysis Evidence

rows: Cochrane meta-analysis; small reduction; role in lung repair
Fischer Pediatrics 2013; Isayama JAMA 2016 (separate noninvasive-strategies review); many EPT still fail CPAP
Askie Cochrane 2017; BUT ↑ mortality + NEC in low-target group
Doyle Cochrane 2014; ↑ CP/death if BPD risk <33%
Doyle Cochrane 2021; less neurotoxic than early high-dose; low-dose preferred
Baud Lancet 2016; ↑ sepsis in <24wk; hydrocortisone 7–14d: no effect on BPD (Onland JAMA 2019)
Yeh AJRCCM 2016; no neuro impairment in limited patients; large trials ongoing

blocks.text: 5.2 Strategies with Inconclusive Evidence
Antenatal steroids: ↓ RDS severity but clinical trials fail to show ↓ BPD; complex interaction: ↑ survival of more preterm → ↑ absolute BPD numbers; transient alveolarization impairment
Exogenous surfactant: ↓ mortality + severe RDS + need for aggressive MV but no ↓ BPD (↑ survival of more preterm); LISA → some ↓ BPD
Caffeine: ↓ BPD (Schmidt NEJM 2006;354:2112) — mechanism: shorter duration MV + anti-inflammatory + diuretic; lacking data from trials with BPD as primary outcome
Volume-targeted ventilation: ↓ duration MV + death or BPD (Klingenberg Cochrane 2017); lacking BPD as primary outcome
Inhaled nitric oxide: inconsistent results; systematic review → no significant effect on BPD (Donohue Pediatrics 2011)
Early PDA closure: prospective RCTs fail to show ↓ BPD
Mesenchymal stem cells: effective in animal models of hyperoxia-induced injury; phase 1 clinical study promising; safety + efficacy in preterm infants needs further study

Steroid Dilemma — BPD Risk Threshold ที่สำคัญ
Doyle meta-regression J Pediatr 2014;165:1258: effect modification by BPD risk: BPD risk <33%: corticosteroids ↑ death or CP; BPD risk >60%: corticosteroids ↓ death or CP. Network meta-analysis (Ramaswamy JAMA Pediatr 2021): dexamethasone 7–14 days at medium cumulative dose 2–4 mg/kg = most effective for preventing BPD — but low-quality evidence + lacking long-term neuro data. ข้อสรุปปฏิบัติ: nebulized budesonide early → ↓ BPD but ↑ mortality (Bassler NEJM 2018) → NOT recommended. Intratracheal budesonide/surfactant (Yeh 2016) → promising, ongoing large trials. Avoid early high-dose dexamethasone; if steroids needed use late low-dose; document BPD risk >60% before initiating.
6. Management of Established BPD
6.1 Respiratory Support
Avoid unnecessary intubation: use NCPAP/NIPPV as primary support; HFNC inferior to NCPAP for initial support (↑ failure rate; Wilkinson Cochrane 2016)
Mechanical ventilation targets: lowest PIP necessary; Vt: avoid <3 mL/kg (atelectasis) or >7 mL/kg (volutrauma); inspiratory time 0.3–0.5 sec; PEEP 4–8 cmH2O
SpO2 target: maintain 90–95% — minimize O2 toxicity while preventing hypoxemia (PH, cor pulmonale risk); increase FiO2 during feedings/agitation
PaCO2 target: avoid >55 mmHg or <40 mmHg during initial period — limit changes in cerebral perfusion; permissive hypercapnia acceptable during weaning if pH acceptable
Weaning: gradual ↓ ventilator rate when: acceptable SpO2 + PaCO2 at low PIP (15–18 cmH2O) + FiO2 <0.3–0.4; caffeine as respiratory stimulant before extubation (Schmidt NEJM 2006)
Established severe BPD with bronchomalacia: higher PEEP 6–10 cmH2O → reduce expiratory airway resistance + improve alveolar ventilation
6.2 Bronchodilator Therapy
Indications: airway smooth muscle hypertrophy + hyperreactivity in severe BPD
β-agonists by inhalation: shown to ↓ airway resistance; no RCTs; effect short-lived; cardiovascular side effects (tachycardia, hypertension, arrhythmias)
Recommendation: limit to acute exacerbations of airway obstruction; avoid chronic use
Maintain adequate oxygenation → prevent hypoxia-induced bronchoconstriction (primary measure)
6.3 Corticosteroid Therapy in Established BPD
Short-term benefits: ↓ inflammation, airway edema, capillary leakage, lung fibrosis; facilitates ventilator weaning
Effects on long-term respiratory outcomes in established BPD: not evaluated in RCTs

Complications of chronic use: mask infection, arterial HTN, hyperglycemia, ↑ proteolysis, adrenocortical suppression, somatic + lung growth suppression, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, ↑ CP/cognitive impairment
Recommendation: limit use; clearly defined goals; risk-benefit analysis each course
7. Pulmonary Hypertension ใน BPD
7.1 Pathophysiology
Prevalence: frequently complicates severe BPD; Mourani PM AJRCCM 2015;191:87 — early pulmonary vascular disease in preterm infants at risk for BPD
Mechanism: ↓ alveolar development → ↓ pulmonary vascular bed; hypoxia → pulmonary vasoconstriction; vascular remodeling (medial hypertrophy); systemic-pulmonary anastomoses → aggravate PH
Pulmonary vascular resistance: extremely sensitive to changes in alveolar PO2 → monitor closely during all activities (feeding, crying, sleep)
7.2 Monitoring
Echocardiogram: close monitoring for early diagnosis; RV hypertrophy, tricuspid regurgitation, RV dysfunction, septal flattening
Pulmonary vein stenosis: acquired complication in severe BPD (Swier Am J Perinatol 2016;33:671); ↑ mortality; regular surveillance proposed
7.3 Management
Oxygen therapy (most important): prevent hypoxemia — maintain SpO2 >90% even during feeding/crying; O2 reduces PVR directly

rows: ↓ PVR in acute PH; no long-term outcome data; no benefit in BPD prevention
Long-term therapy; limited safety/efficacy data in BPD; some clinical reports suggest safe + effective (van der Graaf Pulm Circ 2019)
Also systemic vasodilator; myocardial depression risk
Used when fail initial therapy; efficacy/safety not established in BPD

blocks.text: 8. Fluid, Nutrition และ Infection Management
8.1 Fluid Management
BPD = poor tolerance for fluid overload: ↑ interstitial lung fluid → ↓ PF + ↑ hypoxemia + ↑ CO2 → ↑ MV dependency
Strategy: restrict water + salt to minimum for metabolic needs + growth; diuretics when fluid persists despite restriction
Loop diuretics (furosemide): ↑ lung compliance + ↓ resistance; blood gases not always improve; wide practice variation (Bamat J Pediatr 2021)

Diuretic Complications — รู้ไว้ก่อนสั่ง
1. Ototoxicity (furosemide): especially combined with aminoglycosides. 2. Electrolyte: hypokalemia, hyponatremia, hypochloremia. 3. Metabolic alkalosis: ↑ HCO3, exacerbates respiratory acidosis compensation. 4. Hypercalciuria → nephrocalcinosis: monitor Ca/Cr ratio in urine; supplemental Ca needed. 5. Long-term pulmonary outcomes: not established. Thiazide + spironolactone: alternative to furosemide with fewer electrolyte side effects.
8.2 Nutrition
BPD → impaired growth: ↑ metabolic demand (↑ work of breathing) + steroid/diuretic side effects + frequent feeding interruptions
Consequences of malnutrition: delay alveolar development, ↑ O2-induced lung damage, ↓ cell multiplication, ↑ infection susceptibility
Strategy: aggressive parenteral/oral caloric intake adequate for growth; high-calorie formulas; supplement protein, calcium, phosphorus, zinc; fluid restriction while maintaining growth
Exclusive breast milk: associated with ↓ BPD (Spiegler J Pediatr 2016)
Rickets prevention: rib fractures on CXR + bone demineralization (rickets) common in BPD; cause: ↓ Ca/vitamin D + calciuria from diuretics → supplement Ca + vitamin D
GER: common in BPD; if severe → may contribute to chronic inflammation; antireflux management when documented
Micronutrients (prolonged TPN): monitor vitamins A + E, iron, copper, zinc, selenium (antioxidant + infection + lung repair functions)
Hemoglobin: maintain relatively normal Hgb (↑ metabolic demands → ↑ O2 demand); transfusion or rHuEPO + ↓ lab draws
8.3 Infection Control
Any infection → setback; bacterial/viral/fungal all worsen BPD
Monitor: tracheal secretion culture + Gram stain; ↑ secretion quantity/quality → suspect infection
If pneumonia suspected: CBC, blood culture, CXR → treat based on organism sensitivity
Distinguish colonization vs true infection: avoid over-treatment → ↑ resistant organisms
Prevention measures: hand hygiene, sterile respiratory equipment, isolation from respiratory infections
8.4 Infant Stimulation and Parental Support
Severe BPD → ventilator dependent months → deprived of normal parental stimulation → developmental delays compounded by neuro disability
Structured stimulation program: social, language, cognitive, motor skills; speech therapy for tracheostomy; adaptive tools (beanbag, stroller)
Parents: encourage frequent visits + participation in daily care; education in procedures (chest PT, tracheal suctioning); permanent physician/nursing team for continuity
9. Long-term Outcomes
9.1 Respiratory Outcomes
Mortality: low; when occurs → severe respiratory failure + intercurrent infections + intractable PH + cor pulmonale
Lower respiratory infections: common in childhood; wheezing/bronchiolitis hospitalization frequent first 2 years of life
RSV → potentially life-threatening: palivizumab (RSV prophylaxis) recommended in all infants with BPD during RSV season
Long-term lung function: impaired for many years even if asymptomatic; Northway NEJM 1990;323:1793: original cohort at mean 18 yr → airway obstruction + hyperreactivity + hyperinflation
EPICure study (Fawke AJRCCM 2010;182:237): lung function + respiratory symptoms at 11 yr in extremely preterm — ↓ FEV1, ↑ airflow obstruction, ↑ respiratory symptoms
Simpson Lancet Child Adolesc Health 2018;2:350: lung function trajectories in VLBW survivors: ↓ lung function persists at least through adolescence

Catch-up alveolarization: Narayanan AJRCCM 2013;187:1104 — 3He MRI at 10–14 yr: alveolar dimensions similar to term controls → catch-up alveolarization may occur; lung function trajectory still impaired
Jordan Pediatrics 2020: lung function trajectory set early in life → starting at lower quartile → early onset obstructive lung disease
9.2 Neurodevelopmental Outcomes
Short EJ Pediatrics 2003;112:e359: cognitive + academic consequences at 8 years: 54% of severe BPD survivors required special education vs 37% VLBW without BPD
Neurodevelopmental impairment persists at 8 yr; severity depends on BPD severity + associated risk factors (IVH, PVL, infection)
Diffusing capacity decreased (↓ alveolar-capillary surface); VLBW with BPD → more neuro sequelae than similar VLBW without BPD
10. NICU Practical — การดูแล BPD ใน KCMH NICU
10.1 การตั้ง SpO2 Target
Maintain SpO2 90–95% ที่ baseline; เพิ่ม FiO2 ระหว่าง feeding/agitation/crying
Avoid sustained desaturation <88%: ↑ PVR → ↑ PH + right heart strain
Continuous pulse oximetry เป็น standard; arterial blood gas สำหรับ acute deterioration
PaCO2 target 40–55 mmHg (permissive hypercapnia ยอมได้ถ้า pH >7.25 ขณะ weaning)
10.2 Weaning Checklist
ก่อน extubation: SpO2 stable ≥92% at PIP ≤18 cmH2O + FiO2 ≤0.35 + acceptable PaCO2; caffeine ≥3 days before extubation
หลัง extubation: NCPAP 5–6 cmH2O หรือ NIPPV; monitor closely 24–48h
HFNC ไม่ควรใช้เป็น primary initial support (inferior to NCPAP); อาจ OK เป็น post-extubation step-down
10.3 Echocardiogram Monitoring
Echo ทุก 4–6 wk ใน BPD infants ที่ยัง on respiratory support at ≥32 wk PMA
ดู: TR velocity (estimate RV pressure), RV dysfunction, septal flattening (D-shape), pulmonary vein anatomy
Sildenafil: start 0.5–1 mg/kg/dose q6–8h หาก PH confirmed + O2 therapy ไม่เพียงพอ
10.4 Nutrition Protocol ใน BPD
Target: 130–150 kcal/kg/day; จำกัด fluid 130–150 mL/kg/day
Fortify breast milk ถ้า volume limited; supplement vitamin D 400–1000 IU/day + Ca/phosphorus
Monitor urine Ca/Cr ratio ถ้า on furosemide; target <0.8
Growth chart: weight + length + OFC weekly; detect growth faltering ก่อน discharge planning
10.5 Discharge Planning
Home oxygen: ถ้า SpO2 >90% ใน room air not consistently achievable; instruct family; pulse oximetry at home
Palivizumab: ให้ก่อน RSV season (พฤศจิกายน–มีนาคม) ในทุก BPD infants ที่อายุ <2 ปีตอน RSV season
Multidisciplinary team: neonatologist, pulmonology, cardiology (PH), ophthalmology (ROP), developmental follow-up, speech therapy, OT/PT
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — New BPD = Arrest of Lung Development, Not Just Oxygen Damage
New BPD เกิดจาก alveolar + vascular simplification (Jobe 1999) — ไม่ใช่ fibrosis จาก O2 toxicity แบบ old BPD. ทารก <28 wk อยู่ใน late canalicular/early saccular stage ที่ birth — ปอดยังไม่สร้าง alveoli; ทุก insult (O2, MV, infection, PDA) ขัดขวาง alveolarization → ↓ alveolar number + ↓ capillaries. นี่คือเหตุที่ infants สามารถ survive ด้วย BPD mild ที่ 36 wk แต่ lung function ยังแย่ถึงวัยรุ่น (Simpson 2018). เป้าหมาย prevention: ปกป้อง lung development ไม่ใช่แค่ป้องกัน RDS.

PEARL 2 — SpO2 90–95%: ต้องรักษาทุกกิจกรรม ไม่ใช่แค่ตอนนอน
PVR ใน BPD sensitive มากต่อ alveolar PO2. Hypoxemia ระหว่าง feeding/crying → ↑ PVR → aggravate PH → worsens CO → right heart failure. ดังนั้น: target SpO2 90–95% ต้องมั่นใจว่า maintain ตอน quiet, feeding, AND crying. ถ้า SpO2 ต่ำตอน feeding → เพิ่ม FiO2 ก่อน feed; นัดทำ echo ถ้า PH suspected. Home oxygen discharge: ต้อง SpO2 consistently ≥90% ใน room air ทุก state ก่อน off O2.

PEARL 3 — Steroid Risk Threshold: BPD Risk >60% = Benefit > Harm
Doyle meta-regression (J Pediatr 2014): postnatal corticosteroids ↑ death or CP ถ้า BPD risk <33%; แต่ ↓ death or CP ถ้า BPD risk >60%. ก่อน start steroids ต้อง estimate BPD risk ให้ได้ก่อน — ใช้ clinical prediction tool หรือ >60% ventilated infants at similar GA/weight in center. Network meta-analysis: dexamethasone 7–14 d ที่ cumulative 2–4 mg/kg effective สุด; แต่ evidence quality ต่ำ + ขาด long-term neuro data. ไม่ start early high-dose dexa; prefer late low-dose; document risk threshold ใน chart.

PEARL 4 — Palivizumab เป็น Mandatory ใน BPD <2 ปี ตอน RSV Season
RSV infection ใน BPD → potentially life-threatening; acute bronchiolitis → ↑ airway obstruction + ↑ hyperinflation (difficult to detect on CXR ที่ already hyperinflated) + focal atelectasis. Palivizumab (anti-RSV monoclonal antibody) ลด RSV hospitalization ได้ ~55% (IMpact trial). ในไทย: RSV season ≈ พฤษภาคม–ตุลาคม; ให้ IM ทุก 30 วัน ตลอด season; ทุก BPD infant <2 ปีตอน RSV season ควรได้ palivizumab. ถ้า give ก่อน discharge จาก NICU ยิ่งดี.

PEARL 5 — Diuretics ใน BPD: ใช้ได้ แต่ Monitor Nephrocalcinosis
Furosemide → ↑ lung compliance + ↓ resistance ใน acute phase BPD แต่ long-term role ไม่ชัดเจน; side effects สำคัญ: ototoxicity (combined aminoglycoside), hypokalemia, hypochloremia, metabolic alkalosis, hypercalciuria → nephrocalcinosis. Monitor urine Ca/Cr ratio ทุก 2–4 wk ถ้า chronic furosemide; supplement Ca + vitamin D. ทางเลือก: thiazide + spironolactone → less ototoxic + electrolyte-sparing; ไม่ใช้ chronic furosemide โดยไม่มี clear indication + monitoring plan.
12. Evidence Update — Key Studies

Northway WH NEJM 1967;276:357: first description of BPD in mechanically ventilated preterm infants with severe RDS — coined the term; 4 stages: I (acute), II (regenerative), III (transition), IV (chronic)
Jobe AJ Pediatr Res 1999;46:641: coined 'new BPD' as 'an arrest of lung development' — conceptual shift from injury model to developmental arrest model; framework for current understanding
Bell EF et al JAMA 2022;327:248: NICHD NRN 2013–2018; BPD incidence ~40% in <28wk survivors to 36wk PMA; stable or slightly increasing trend vs 2008
Hillman NH Am J Resp Crit Care Med 2007;176:575: brief large Vt ventilation in fetal sheep: initiates lung injury + systemic inflammatory response — established volutrauma as key BPD mechanism
Schmidt B NEJM 2006;354:2112: CAP trial: caffeine therapy for apnea of prematurity → significant ↓ BPD incidence (36% vs 47%), ↑ neurodevelopmental outcome at 18–21 months
Fischer HS Pediatrics 2013;132:e1351: early CPAP → OR 0.83 (0.71–0.96) BPD or death vs invasive MV; many EPT infants still fail CPAP
Isayama T JAMA 2016;316:611: comparative review of noninvasive ventilation strategies (CPAP, NIPPV, HFNC) for preterm respiratory support
Askie LM Cochrane 2017;CD011190: SpO2 target: lower target → RR 0.87 (0.81–0.94) BPD but ↑ mortality + NEC; critical balance needed; consensus 90–95% for established BPD
Baud O Lancet 2016;387:1827 (PREMILOC): early low-dose hydrocortisone → ↑ survival without BPD (OR 1.48 [1.02–2.16]); ↑ sepsis in <24wk; no long-term neuro benefit shown (JAMA 2017)
Onland W JAMA 2019;321:354: hydrocortisone 7–14 days in MV preterm: no improvement in death or BPD at 36wk PMA — does not support hydrocortisone for this indication
Yeh TF Am J Resp Crit Care Med 2016;193:86: intratracheal budesonide + surfactant: RR 0.58 (0.44–0.77) BPD; no adverse neuro outcomes in limited patients; large multicenter trials ongoing
Bassler D NEJM 2018;378:148: inhaled budesonide from day 1: ↓ BPD but ↑ mortality → NOT recommended
Doyle LW J Pediatr 2014;165:1258: meta-regression: postnatal corticosteroids increase death/CP if BPD risk <33%; decrease if BPD risk >60% — threshold for treatment decision

Ramaswamy VV JAMA Pediatr 2021;175:e206826: network meta-analysis postnatal corticosteroids: dexamethasone 7–14 d at 2–4 mg/kg cumulative = most effective — low quality evidence; lacks long-term neuro data
Darlow BA Cochrane 2016;CD000501: vitamin A supplementation VLBW: RR 0.87 (0.77–0.99) BPD — small but significant; supports supplementation
Mourani PM AJRCCM 2015;191:87: early pulmonary vascular disease in preterm at BPD risk — early echocardiographic assessment important for surveillance
Lapcharoensap W JAMA Pediatr 2015;169:e143676: wide hospital variation in BPD incidence — quality improvement opportunity; center practices significantly modifiable
Short EJ Pediatrics 2003;112:e359: 8-year outcomes: 54% BPD severe required special education vs 37% VLBW without BPD; cognitive + academic impairment persists
Northway WH NEJM 1990;323:1793: 18-year follow-up original 1967 cohort: airway obstruction + hyperreactivity + hyperinflation — lung damage lifelong
Simpson SJ Lancet Child Adolesc Health 2018;2:350: lung function trajectories VLBW survivors: impaired at least through adolescence; early deficit → persistent deficit
13. Self-Check Q&A
Q: NICHD 2018 definition ของ BPD: mild vs moderate vs severe แตกต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Mild BPD: noninvasive PPV หรือ NC ≥3 L/min @ 21% FiO2 OR NC 1–3 L/min @ 22–29% OR NC <1 L/min @ 22–70%. Moderate BPD: invasive PPV @ 21% OR noninvasive PPV/NC ≥3 L/min @ 22–29% OR NC ≥1 L/min/hood @ ≥30% OR NC 1 L/min @ >70%. Severe BPD: invasive PPV @ >21% OR noninvasive PPV/NC ≥3 L/min @ ≥30%. All assessed at 36 wk PMA. Limitations: non-pulmonary causes (central apnea) may be classified; infants dying before 36 wk excluded.
Q: อธิบาย 'new BPD' vs 'old BPD' — ต่างกันอย่างไรในด้าน pathology, clinical, radiograph?

ตอบ: Old BPD: more mature infants; severe RDS → high pressure/O2 MV; pathology = emphysema + fibrosis + atelectasis; CXR = hyperinflation + dense areas; high mortality + cor pulmonale. New BPD: extremely preterm <28 wk; milder initial RF; pathology = ↓ + simplified alveoli, ↓ dysmorphic capillaries (arrest of lung development — Jobe 1999); CXR = diffuse haziness; lower mortality but significant long-term morbidity. Jobe AJRCCM 2001 coined term to distinguish these phenotypes.
Q: ทำไม early CPAP ถึงไม่ prevent BPD ได้ทั้งหมด? และ HFNC มีที่ทางไหนใน BPD prevention?

ตอบ: Early CPAP: meta-analysis (Fischer Pediatrics 2013): OR 0.83 (0.71–0.96) BPD or death — benefit มีแต่น้อย; ปัญหาหลัก: infants <26 wk จำนวนมาก fail CPAP → ต้องใช้ invasive MV. NIPPV ผล inconsistent ระหว่าง trials (Kirpalani NEJM 2013). HFNC: inferior to NCPAP เป็น primary respiratory support — ↑ treatment failure + trend toward ↑ BPD (Wilkinson Cochrane 2016); อาจเป็น step-down post-extubation แต่ไม่ใช้แทน CPAP ตอนแรก.
Q: Postnatal steroid threshold: เมื่อไหร่จึงควร start dexamethasone สำหรับ BPD prevention?

ตอบ: Doyle meta-regression J Pediatr 2014: BPD risk <33% → steroids ↑ death or CP; BPD risk >60% → steroids ↓ death or CP. Ramaswamy network meta-analysis 2021: dexamethasone 7–14 d cumulative 2–4 mg/kg most effective. ปฏิบัติ: ไม่ start early high-dose; use late (≥7 days) low-dose; ประเมิน BPD risk ก่อน — center-specific prediction tool; document risk threshold ใน chart; limit duration; reassess neurologic outcome data.
Q: BPD + Pulmonary Hypertension: จะ workup และ treat อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Workup: echo ทุก 4–6 wk ใน MV BPD; ดู TR velocity + RV function + septal shape + pulmonary vein anatomy (rule out acquired PVS). Management step: (1) O2 therapy (most important): maintain SpO2 >90% ALL activities; (2) iNO: acute PH; (3) sildenafil: chronic PH, 0.5–1 mg/kg q6–8h; (4) nifedipine: use caution (systemic vasodilator + myocardial depression); (5) ET antagonists/prostacyclin: rescue. Pulmonary vein stenosis = separate complication → worse prognosis → refer cardiac surgery.
Q: Caffeine: ทำงานอย่างไรใน BPD prevention และ evidence level ใด?

ตอบ: Schmidt B NEJM 2006;354:2112 (CAP trial): caffeine for apnea of prematurity → ↓ BPD: 36% vs 47%, RR significantly reduced; also ↑ neurodevelopmental outcome at 18–21 months. Mechanisms: ↓ duration of MV (shorter exposure to volutrauma), ↑ respiratory drive, anti-inflammatory effects, mild diuretic effect. Current status: listed as strategy with inconclusive evidence only because no RCT with BPD as primary outcome — but CAP trial remains strong secondary evidence; practice: start caffeine early in all EPT.
Q: จะ monitor และ prevent rickets ใน infant ที่ on chronic furosemide สำหรับ BPD?

ตอบ: Mechanism: furosemide → ↑ renal Ca excretion → hypercalciuria → nephrocalcinosis + osteopenia/rickets. Risk ↑ if prolonged use + ↓ nutritional Ca/vitamin D. Monitor: urine Ca/Cr ratio q2–4 wk (normal <0.8); serum Ca, P, ALP, 25-OH vitamin D; rib fractures on CXR. Management: elemental Ca supplement 150–200 mg/kg/day; vitamin D 800–1000 IU/day; consider switching to thiazide + spironolactone (less calciuric); minimize furosemide course duration.
Q: Long-term prognosis ของ BPD: เด็ก BPD จะมี respiratory outcome อย่างไรถึงวัยผู้ใหญ่?

ตอบ: Northway NEJM 1990: original cohort at 18 yr: airway obstruction + hyperreactivity + hyperinflation. EPICure (Fawke AJRCCM 2010): ↓ FEV1 + ↑ airway obstruction at 11 yr. Simpson Lancet 2018: lung function trajectories impaired through adolescence. Jordan Pediatrics 2020: lung function trajectory set early → starting at lower quartile → early onset COPD. BUT: Narayanan AJRCCM 2013 — 3He MRI at 10–14 yr: catch-up alveolarization may occur (alveolar dimensions normalize); function still impaired. ดังนั้น: lung structure recovers partially; lung function trajectory may not — emphasizes importance of BPD prevention.
Q: อธิบาย role ของ vitamin A ใน BPD — ทำงานอย่างไร และ evidence ดีแค่ไหน?

ตอบ: Role: vitamin A (retinol) critical for lung development + repair of respiratory epithelium after injury; ↓ levels found in infants who develop BPD. Evidence (Darlow Cochrane 2016;CD000501): vitamin A IM 5,000 IU x3/week x4 weeks → RR 0.87 (0.77–0.99) for BPD or death — small but statistically significant reduction. Mechanism: ↑ retinyl palmitate in lung tissue → regulate gene expression for lung maturation + repair + antioxidant defense + ↑ production of secretory IgA. Current use: strategy with RCT evidence in Table 70.2; largely replaced by other prevention priorities but still valid especially in resource-limited settings.
Q: Intratracheal budesonide + surfactant: ทำงานอย่างไร และอยู่ที่ stage ใดของ evidence?

ตอบ: Rationale: surfactant เป็น carrier สำหรับ budesonide → deliver steroid directly to lung parenchyma → ↓ local inflammation + alveolar injury; surfactant เปิด collapsed alveoli → ↑ drug distribution; systemic absorption ต่ำกว่า systemic dexamethasone. Yeh AJRCCM 2016;193:86 (RCT): RR 0.58 (0.44–0.77) BPD; no adverse neurodevelopmental outcomes in limited patients. Current status: RCT evidence available but limited patient numbers + no long-term neuro data; large multicenter trials underway. Listed in Table 70.2 with RR 0.58 — promising strategy but await large trial results before widespread adoption.

Day 168 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.71
บทต่อไป: Chapter 71 — Postneonatal Respiratory Morbidity. เตรียมตัว: lung function trajectories after preterm birth ใน infancy → childhood → adolescence; obstructive vs restrictive patterns, reversibility; respiratory morbidity (recurrent wheeze, asthma, respiratory infections); catch-up lung growth vs impaired airway development; factors that predict worse postneonatal outcomes; surveillance strategies; transition from NICU respiratory care to pediatric pulmonology follow-up.
